name: sdlc-plugin
description: Software Development Lifecycle skills, bundled. Fifteen skills covering bootstrap → solution design → platform → requirements → tasks → implementation, plus audit and course-correction utilities: /repo-bootstrap (new GitHub repo from scratch), /solution-design (first-stab combined business + technical solution view written to docs/design/solution-design.md — 15 sections covering purpose, users, value, key features, high-level user flows, major screens/surfaces at thinking-level, system context, components, solution strategy, data approach, integrations, NFR drivers, constraints, risks; grounded in C4 Context+Container, Arc42, TOGAF Architecture Vision, Lean Canvas, Jobs-to-be-Done, AWS Well-Architected, IEEE 42010), /platform-design (deeper architectural design into docs/architecture/ — system type, components, hosting, stack, data, integrations, lightweight ADRs, open questions; reads solution-design as the upstream input), /platform-provision (stands up the cloud/SaaS platforms the architecture names — hosting, observability, DBs, auth, email, etc.; tries every available channel including connected MCP servers, CLIs, and HTTP APIs; batches human-only steps like account creation and secret pasting into one checklist; wires resulting secrets into GitHub Actions and platform env stores; records everything in docs/architecture/provisioning-log.md), /platform-verify (smoke-tests + security-audits the provisioned platform — reachability via MCP/CLI/API, secret hygiene, CI wiring, security posture including branch protection, secret scanning, dependabot, least-privilege tokens; appends verdict to the provisioning log), /repo-release-ready (release/uat + release/prod branches, promotion workflows, secret scanning, branch-protection rulesets — runs after the platform exists so deploy gates reference real provisioned infra), /requirements-create-from-design (interactively elicit functional + non-functional requirements into docs/requirements/ — prefers docs/design/solution-design.md as the source, falls back to README.md, then interactive; reads docs/architecture/ if present so requirements respect the recorded architecture), /requirements-validation (validate and advance the status of existing requirements; five-pass model — statement accuracy, fit-criterion measurability, assumption hold, open-question resolution, INCOSE quality + smell list; cascades on falsified assumptions), /tasks-create-from-requirements (turn the requirements into a staged plan of small-batch GitHub issues with milestones, labels, and human-required tasks front-loaded), /task-implement (heavyweight end-to-end implementation of one GitHub issue via a multi-persona orchestration — Principal Engineer, QA Engineer, Cloud Architect, UX/UI Designer, Test Automation Engineer, Project Manager, and Work Checker — all reading docs/architecture/ before their phases; audit-trail comments, diligent PM bounce-back gate, Playwright-based UX verification), /status-help (analyses repo state and recommends a single concrete next step in the SDLC workflow — recognises which skills have run and which still need to; useful when you're in the middle of a multi-step project and unsure what to do next), /requirements-add (add a single new requirement to an existing requirements tree — duplicate-checks, conflict-checks, decides interactively with the user whether to extend an existing file or create a new one, then elaborates the requirement in detail and updates the cascade), /requirements-delete (remove or demote a single requirement — scans the whole tree for references so nothing silently breaks, surfaces any GitHub issues that implement it, then executes only after explicit approval of the full change set), /ai-check-work (generic 'please just check your work carefully' second-pass skill that re-examines whatever was just produced — code, plans, writing, analysis, anything — and reports issues specifically; saves the user typing the phrase), /requirements-rework (when an implementation-time discovery invalidates the plan — assertively cleans up stale requirements and issues, with a documented audit trail).
